creator: trevor@jpj.net
text:
Thank you--applying your patches fixed this port for me.

> 	I don't know why I did not notice it before :( The fonts.alias
> 	file was generated with ``#'' instead of ``!'' as the comment,
> 	which prevented X-server from using the directory.  The port's
> 	instructions in pkg-message had a typo too...

The instruction for use of xset is still incorrect, because with the fp+
option the new font path(s) must be specified.  I suggest something like:

	For your X server to use the fonts, you have to add
	their directory to the font path.  If your system is
	typical, add a suitable FontPath line to /etc/XF86Config
	and restart the X server, or do:

		xset fp+ /usr/X11R6/lib/X11/fonts/koi8-u-gemini

	(the first part of the path may vary) to enable the fonts
	just for the current X session.

If the fonts could go in an existing directory--say
${X11BASE}/lib/X11/fonts/cyrillic/--then the instructions could be
simplified:  just "xset fp rehash" or a restart of the server should be
enough.

> 		. use ``-j 2'' during build -- this speeds up somewhat
> 		  even on non-SMP machines.

I'm not sure about this.  I have a feeling this should ordinarily be set
by the user in /etc/make.conf (maybe -j 8 would work best on the user's
8-way system), and only set by ports when it is necessary.  I also have a
feeling that if a port needed to set this, it would more likely set "-j 1"
and maybe "-B".
-- 
Trevor Johnson
http://jpj.net/~trevor/gpgkey.txt
